text.*:
"Reaper" redirects here. For the title, see the Reaper.
For bosses that can be killed to fulfil a slayer assignment, see Slayer#Boss slayer.
Soul Reaper
Release date 18 August 2014 (Update)
Location Death's office
Members Yes
Currency Reaper points, Slayer points
[view] • [talk]

Soul Reaper, also known as Boss Slayer or Reaper Task, is an activity that helps players train Slayer by killing boss monsters. The player receives assignments from Death, and then kills bosses to harvest their souls, which award Slayer experience, slayer points, and reaper points that can be spent on various items and permanent effects at Death's Store, including hydrix gems.

Soul Reaper assignments require level 45 Slayer and a combat level of 50.

Getting a task

[edit | edit source]

To start the activity, the player must visit Death's office via Death's Hourglass next to the Draynor lodestone. The player can then talk to Death to obtain a grim gem and get assigned their first task. After the first task, further ones can be obtained by using the grim gem, making travelling back to his office unnecessary apart from collecting rewards.

Reaper tasks can be assigned and completed indefinitely, with no daily limit. However, players can earn up to a maximum of 300 reaper points per week, resetting on Wednesday at 00:00 UTC. Points earned that would put the player above the cap are forfeit. Reaper tasks can still be taken and completed after reaching this cap, but no additional reaper points will be awarded. Reaper tasks also award Slayer points for every task, equal to the amount of Slayer points you would have received from your highest available Slayer Master (22 at level 120+ combat and 90+ Slayer, with Smoking Kills completed); Slayer points are not capped.

While Death is kidnapped by Sliske during the events of Missing, Presumed Death, his clanmate Frank fulfils his role in the activity for him for the duration of the quest.

Rerolling

[edit | edit source]

Reaper tasks can be rerolled by spending 30 Slayer points per reroll. This fee can be waived by having a Soul Reaper Refresh in the backpack while asking Death to reroll an assignment.

Boss selection

[edit | edit source]

By purchasing the "Reaper's Choice" unlock (250 reaper points), there is a 10% chance that Death will allow the player to pick a boss of their choice, rather than assigning a random boss.

Unlocking the various tiers of War's Blessing will additively increase the chance of triggering Reaper's Choice: 5% at tier 1, 10% at tier 2, 25% at tier 3, and 100% at tier 4.

Assignments

[edit | edit source]

Death (or Frank) may assign the following bosses, with numbers depending on the difficulty of the boss, as long as the player meets all the requirements.

In the Slayer section of Settings under Skills & Experience, players can toggle:

  1. Enabling group bosses (the Ambassador; the Barrows: Rise of the Six; black stone dragon; Kalphite King; Nex; Nex: Angel of Death; Seiryu, the Azure Serpent; Solak, Guardian of the Grove; Vorago; and Zamorak, Lord of Chaos) as possible assignments;
  2. Enabling skilling bosses (Croesus and the Gate of Elidinis) as possible assignments;
  3. Extending the amount of bosses. This simply doubles the amount that is normally assigned.

Selecting either group bosses or extended tasks increases the number of reaper points rewarded for all tasks by 25%, even for solo boss assignments when group assignments are enabled. Selecting both options increases the number of reaper points rewarded by roughly 50% (the bonus points for both +25% are rounded down before adding to base, example: 23 to 28 to 33 or 10 to 12 to 14). Players can toggle these options only when they are not on an assignment.

Unless War's blessing 4 is obtained with Reaper's Choice, it is not recommended to toggle both group and extended tasks, as it is likely to be assigned a group boss in which the player may not be able to complete for various reasons. Afterwards, both can be toggled as the player can pick the task they want. For the fastest reaper point gain at the cost of more slayer points, players should activate group bosses and disable extended tasks. The 25% reduction in points is made up for with the ability to clear more tasks, especially in lower count tasks such as TzKal-Zuk or Nakatra, Devourer Eternal. For most players it is advised to leave group assignments and extended tasks disabled.

Unlike normal Slayer:

  • Players do not receive Slayer experience for every boss killed. It is instead awarded at the end of the assignment.
  • Players only need to deal a small amount of damage to a boss for it to count for the task, as opposed to the majority of it.
  • The usual bonuses from the slayer helmet do not work on Soul Reaper tasks, unless the player has a regular slayer assignment for the same monster.

After each kill, the player is notified how many they have left for the task. Upon completing the task, the player is awarded with a lump sum of Slayer experience, Slayer points, and some reaper points. Every 10 tasks completed awards an additional 10 reaper points, while every 50 tasks completed awards an extra 50 reaper points. The higher point reward overrides the lesser one; at 100 tasks completed, the player would get an extra 50 reaper points instead of 60. No reaper points will be awarded for a task streak if the weekly cap has been reached.

If the player has reached the cap of 16,000 reaper points and has not yet reached their weekly cap, completing another reaper task will count towards their weekly cap, but the points will be lost.

Bosses that possess a harder variant, such as hard or challenge mode (not successors, like Nex: Angel of Death), provide additional Slayer experience and reaper points. However, the entire task must be done on hard mode.

If a deathtouched dart is used to defeat a boss, the kill will not count towards the task, as it does not register on the Beasts tab. This effect will also persist for 15 minutes after throwing the dart.

While on a Reaper assignment, custom-fit masterwork equipment will not degrade until the task is completed.

  7. ^ The kill is registered when all of the brothers (except Akrisae and Linza) are killed and the chest is looted. Therefore, the player cannot simply kill six brothers if the task is to kill six; the player must instead kill all of them and loot the chest six times.
  8. ^ It is not necessary to kill all three Kings for each kill.
  9. ^ TzekHaar-Aken doesn't count.
  10. ^ Exiled Kalphite Queen counts.
  11. ^ a b Requires completion of Gielinor's Most Expensive Doorstop to select with Reaper's choice.
  12. ^ TokHaar and TzekHaar-Jad don't count.

Rewards

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Death's Store

Players can access the reward shop, Death's Store, only by speaking with him in person. It is not accessible via the grim gem.

In addition to Slayer experience, players also are rewarded with slayer points and reaper points, which are used to purchase items and effects. Most of these can also be purchased with 250 slayer points. There are also cosmetic rewards that are unlocked by completing certain objectives. Unlocking all permanent effects is a requirement for the trimmed completionist cape. The Final Boss title is not required for the trimmed completionist cape.

It is possible to obtain Crabbe, the Slayer pet when completing an assignment and receiving Slayer experience.

Each reaper point is worth 127,821 coins, based on the median GE price of an incomplete hydrix (or 69,936 coins based on death notes). Since some rewards can be purchased with either reaper points or slayer points, one of those two options will usually be cheaper because of the different opportunity costs. The value of a slayer point (based on the value of Slayer Dart runes) is 1,364 coins, so for example the bonfire upgrade effectively costs 31,955,178 coins if purchased with reaper points, and only 3,410,714 coins if purchased with slayer points.

    Update history

    [edit | edit source]
    This information has been compiled as part of the update history project. Some updates may not be included – see here for how to help out!
    • patch 23 March 2026 (Update):
      • Fixed an issue where players would lose slayer points if they were at the max points cap and completed a reaper task.
    • hotfix 19 March 2026:
      • Jad and Har-Aken variants (such as from a Zuk encounter) no longer count towards the respective reaper tasks.
    • hotfix 17 March 2026:
      • Adjusted Soul Reaper XP gained when completing Jad and Har-Aken tasks via the TzKal-Zuk encounter (now 25% of previous values) to address unintended reset behaviour.
    • update 16 March 2026 (Update):
      • Soul Reaper no longer has a daily task cap.
      • The free daily reroll is no longer available.
      • Reaper tasks can now be assigned and completed indefinitely, with no daily limit - Instead, a new Reaper Points cap of 300 points per week has been implemented.
      • Reaper tasks can still be taken and completed after reaching this cap, but no additional reaper points will be given.
      • Reaper tasks now also reward Slayer points alongside reaper points. Slayer points are not capped.
      • Reaper tasks can now be reset using 30 Slayer points (this replaces the previous reroll system).
      • Reaper assignments now require level 45 Slayer and an overall combat level of 50 and have also generally been rebalanced with a view towards task length, reaper points and XP.
        • See Dailyscape Overhaul#Soul Reaper for full list of changes.
    • patch 5 August 2024 (Update):
      • Fixed an issue that prevented 'Nakatra, Devourer Eternal' from being correctly set as a hard mode Reaper kill if the boss was killed in hard mode.
      • The boss will now correctly award the following for a hard mode Reaper kill:
        • 30,000 Slayer XP
        • 25 Reaper Points (at a base level, this may be increased with boons or unlocks)
    • update 22 July 2024 (Update):
      • New boss Nakatra, Devourer Eternal added with the launch of the Sanctum of Rebirth
    • hidden 27 February 2023 (Update):
      • Reduced the Slayer experience and reaper points awarded for Croesus reaper tasks.
        • Slayer experience from 20,000 to 10,000 per task.
        • Reaper points from 22, 27, 32 to 11, 13, 15 points for normal; extended or group; and extended and group tasks respectively.
    • ninja 18 July 2022 (Update):
      • Reduced Soul Reaper task of Zamorak, Lord of Chaos from 1-4 (2-8 extended) to 2-3 (4-6 extended).
    • patch 1 November 2021 (Update):
      • Fixed an issue with TzekHaar-Jad Souls not counting towards the Reaper Task.
    • patch 13 September 2021 (Update):
      • Reaper Assignments will now correctly indicate that the Arch-Glacor is the target when using quick-chat.
      • Fixed an issue that caused some of the boss buttons on the Reaper's Choice list to be disabled even when players met the requirements.
      • Raids bosses and the first two bosses of each Elite Dungeon are once again hidden in Reaper's Choice, and cannot be assigned as a Reaper Task.
      • Fixed an issue where all subsequent bosses in the Reaper's Choice interface after the first locked boss were also locked.
      • Death once again ignores 'soft conditions' on bosses when determining whether he can assign them as a Reaper Task. For example, Death will assign the Magister provided you have 115 Slayer even if you don't have any Keys to the Crossing. Soft conditions include acquirable items like Barrows Totems, Ascension Keystones, etc.
      • Corrected an issue where Song from the Depths was unintentionally a requirement for Death to assign Queen Black Dragon as a Reaper Task.
    • patch 6 September 2021 (Update):
      • Slayer and Reaper tasks in the Activity Tracker now include a 'Weakness' field in their description which highlights the most effective combat style. During a task, an icon for this weakness will replace its Activity Tracker icon.
      • The text which appears when hovering over the Slayer Counter if you are able to capture the target's soul and have not yet done so will now also appear in the Activity Tracker description for that task.
      • Slayer and Reaper tasks in the Activity Tracker now continue to display the name of the target when there are no kills left, instead of displaying 'no task'. This makes it consistent with the Slayer/Reaper counter.
      • Slayer and Reaper tasks in the Activity Tracker will now refresh correctly if the target changes but the number of kills left remains the same.
    • hotfix 13 September 2021 (Update):
      • Death will no longer assign bosses he shouldn't.
    • patch 17 February 2020 (Update):
      • Players can no longer receive the first two bosses from Elite Dungeons as Reaper assignments.
    • patch 20 January 2020 (Update):
      • Fixed an issue stopping Death from allowing players to reroll Reaper tasks.
    • patch 5 August 2019 (Update):
      • Removed a comma from the "Seiryu the Azure Serpent" in the reaper task interface and dialogue.
    • patch 20 May 2019 (Update):.
      • Both God Wars Dungeon 2 and Elite Dungeon bosses now award more Reaper points and Slayer XP when killed in challenge/hard mode.
    • update 25 February 2019 (Update):
      • Increased slayer experience and reaper point rewards from Elite Dungeon reaper assignments.
      • Allowed Death to assign the Elite Dungeon's final boss as a potential reaper assignment without having to rely on the "Reaper's Choice" perk.
    • patch 8 October 2018 (Update):
      • Fixed an issue with the settings refresh that caused Group reaper assignments setting to be inverted.
    • patch 4 June 2018 (Update):
      • The minimum and maximum amount of reaper kills assigned for Solak has been lowered.
    • patch 4 September 2017 (Update):
      • The base Reaper points received from completing bosses in the Heart of Gielinor in Challenger mode have been increased by 2.
      • The base Reaper points received from defeating Telos has been increased by 1.
    • patch 20 March 2017 (Update):
      • Players will no longer lose slayer points if they click out of the task selection interface after purchasing an additional Soul Reaper task.
    • hotfix 23 January 2017:
      • An achievement to unlock all permanent effects from Soul Reaper has been added as a requirement for the trimmed completionist cape.[1]
    • patch 23 January 2017 (Update):
      • Players will no longer be assigned the Queen Black Dragon from Death without 60 Summoning.
    • patch 17 October 2016 (Update):
      • Slayer XP rewarded from completing Reaper tasks as an Ironman is no longer halved.
    • patch 8 September 2014 (Update):
      • Hydrix jewellery can now be repaired with onyx. You can now use a cut onyx on any enchanted hydrix jewellery to recharge it by 50% per gem.
      • The cost of buying additional tasks has been reduced from 500 to 250 slayer points.
      • The cost of an incomplete hydrix has been reduced from 400 to 300 reaper points.
      • You can now obtain multiple grim gems.
      • There is now a total Slayer creature kills counter on the Slayer kills tab. This is the sum of kills for all the tracked monsters.
      • There is now a new Boss Kills tab similar to the Slayer kills tab that displays all boss kills along with a total.
      • Players can now request to have their assignment size doubled to earn an extra 25% reward points. The option for this can be found in the 'Options' tab on Death's reward interface.
      • Quick Chat for Soul Reaper can now be found in the Skills > Slayer section.
      • Death and Frank now have right-click options for getting an assignment or viewing rewards when you visit them via Death's portal.
      • Choosing to reroll a Soul Reaper task on a new day will no longer stop you from being able to receive another task that day.
      • Har-Aken will no longer be assigned as a Soul Reaper task to those that are yet to sacrifice a fire cape.
      • The rewards for God Wars bosses on the Beasts interface no longer shows combined godsword shards.
      • There is no longer a 'Fastest Kill Time' icon in the Beasts interface for bosses that do not store this information (Barrows; Corporeal Beast; Chaos Elemental; Dagannoth Kings; Kalphite Queen; King Black Dragon).
    • patch 26 August 2014 (Update):
      • Killing Commander Zilyana in hard mode will now count towards Soul Reaper assignments correctly.
    • patch 18 August 2014 (Update):
      • Added to game.

    Trivia

    [edit | edit source]
    • As Death is kidnapped by Sliske during the events of Missing, Presumed Death, his clanmate Frank fulfils his role in the activity for him for the duration of the quest.
    • Reaper points obtained by completing assignments received a 50% bonus after the release of War's Retreat on 27 January 2020 until the end of 9 February 2020.
      • This was notified with the login message War's recent return has inspired Death to reward 50% bonus reaper points. Be sure to visit War in his new retreat near the Draynor lodestone! on 28 January 2020.
      • When points were obtained, the following message was shown: War's recent return inspires Death to reward 50% bonus reaper points.
    • Reaper points were again increased when the alchemical hydrix was released on 17 August 2020 until the end of 31 August 2020. This was notified with the login and reward message: The Alchemical Hydrix rewards have inspired Death to reward 50% bonus reaper points.
    • From 10 February 2020 the player was able to be assigned the non-final boss of Elite Dungeons as reaper assignments. Although these tasks provided descriptions from Death, it was confirmed to be a bug.[2] They gave the same number of reaper points as the final boss would and were not included in the Reaper's choice list. This was later fixed in an update on 17 February 2020.
      • This bug reappeared again during the 6 September 2021 update.
    • The Arch-Glacor used to award the same points and experience as in normal mode even if the task was fully completed in hard mode. This was quickly changed in an unknown update.

    References

    [edit | edit source]
    1. ^ Mod Wolf. Hotfix - Reaper Trim Requirements. Reddit. 23 January 2017. (Archived from the original on 17 April 2019.)
    2. ^ Jagex. Mod Breezy's Twitter account. 11 February 2020. (Archived from the original on 4 November 2020.) Mod Breezy: "Its a bug i saw Mod Camel working on today"

properties.name: description
properties.*: Soul Reaper, also known as Boss Slayer or Reaper Task, is an activity that helps players train Slayer by killing boss monsters. The player receives assignments from Death, and then kills bosses to harvest their souls, which award Slayer experience, slayer points, and reaper points that can be spent on...
